title: 5.3.4 Floyd Algorithm
subtitle: All-pairs shortest path via dynamic programming

content.introduction: Floyd (Floyd-Warshall) computes all-pairs shortest paths through dynamic programming over intermediate vertex sets.

sections.title: 5.3.4.1 Key Ideas and Model
sections.content: Define DP state dist[i][j] and transition via intermediate node k: min(dist[i][j], dist[i][k] + dist[k][j]).
examples: Initialize distance matrix with direct edges and infinity

sections.title: 5.3.4.2 Representation and Workflow
sections.content: Describe matrix initialization and triple-loop update order for all vertices as intermediate candidates.
examples: Apply one k-iteration and update affected pairs

sections.title: 5.3.4.3 Complexity and Practical Guidance
sections.content: Complexity is O(V^3) time and O(V^2) space; discuss suitability for dense graphs and full pairwise analysis.
examples: Use Floyd for offline all-pairs planning

keyTakeaways: Compute shortest paths between every node pair
Evaluate O(V^3) and O(V^2) resource costs
Use Floyd for dense or full-pair requirements
